msContents.summary: Maḥzor for the entire year according to the Western Ashkenazi rite

msItem.title: מחזור מנהג אשכנז המערבי לכל השנה
Maḥzor minhag Ashkenaz ha-maʿaravi le-khol ha-shanah
msItem.note: The third of three volumes; the other two volumes are MS Michael 571 (Neubauer 1097; vol. 1) and MS Michael 569 (Neubauer 1098; vol. 2).
Used by Daniel Goldschmidt in his edition of the Sukkot services (Jerusalem, 1981).

supportDesc.condition: The bottom margin of f. 25r has been repaired with a parchment strip taken from a Latin manuscript from the late-13th or early-14th century.

handDesc.handNote: On 1v, 3r, 24r, 32r, the name יצחק (Isaac) has been picked out by the scribal hand. In some of the reshuyot for Simḥat Torah (ff. 27v, 29r) and for the bridegroom in the wedding ceremony (ff. 131v, 132r), the scribe inserted the name Isaac b. Samuel, which was most likely either his full name or the name of the patron.

decoDesc.p: Penwork decorations in black and red around initial words, including arched doorways (f. 1r), roundels (ff. 10r, 49r), rectangular frames (f. 17r), drawings of animals (f. 10r), as well as a drawing of a Jew holding the lulav (f. 21v).
Some of the commentary text has been copied in the form of carmina figurata (see for exaple ff. 4r, 49r).

bindingDesc.p: Golden-brown fine-grained leather over paper boards; the current shelfmark has been gold-tooled and painted (in black and white) on the spine.

note.p: The dating is based on the calendar on f. 95r in MS Michael 569, which begins mid-cycle from the year 5049 (1288/9).

history.provenance: In the late-sixteenth century, all three volumes of this Maḥzor were owned by members of the Ḳoser קוצר family, including Meshullam b. Mordecai Ḳoser משולם בן מרדכי קוצר and his brothers Joseph יוסף (MS Michael 571, f. 8v) and Menaḥem מנחם who recorded the birth of his son in 1582 (MS Michael 573, f. 45v). This is possibly the same as the Ḳoser קוצר family in Riva del Garda, where a tombstone belonging to one Meshullam Ḳoser משולם קוצר who died in 1541 has survived, while a Mordecai Ḳoser מרדכי קוצר is attested in 1564 (see Kaufmann 1895, 1896, 1897). These may have been the grandfather and father of the owners who left inscriptions in the manuscript.
history.acquisition: It belonged to the library of Heimann Joseph Michael (1792-1846), which was purchased en bloc (altogether 862 volumes) by the Bodleian Library in 1848 through Messrs. Asher and Co. for £1030.

recordHist.source: Manuscript description based on Catalogue of the Hebrew Manuscripts in the Bodleian Library, vol. I, by Adolf Neubauer, Oxford 1886, No. 1099, Catalogue of the Hebrew manuscripts in the Bodleian Library; Supplement of Addenda and Corrigenda to Vol. I, No. 1099, and on the data of the Institute of Microfilmed Hebrew Manuscripts, National Library of Israel with additional enhancements by the cataloguer.

availability.p: Entry to read in the Library is permitted only on presentation of a valid reader's card (for admissions procedures contact Bodleian Admissions). Contact specialcollections.enquiries@bodleian.ox.ac.uk for further information on the availability of this manuscript.

listBibl.bibl: Neubauer, Adolf, Catalogue of the Hebrew Manuscripts in the Bodleian Library (Oxford, 1886), no. 1099.
Kaufmann, David, 'L'inscription à Riva', Revue des études juives 31:62 (1895): 292-293.
Kaufmann, David, 'La pierre tumulaire de Meschoullam Kocer à Riva', Revue des études juives 33:66 (1896): 311-314.
Kaufmann, David, 'La famille קוצר ou Cousseri à Riva', Revue des études juives 35:70 (1897): 302-304.

Beit-Arié, Malachi and R.A. May (eds), Catalogue of the Hebrew manuscripts in the Bodleian Library; Supplement of Addenda and Corrigenda to Vol. I (A. Neubauer’s Catalogue) (Oxford: Clarendon Press, 1994), no. 1099.
